case_title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION INC.,DBA GREAT VS. BLUE FLOWER LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY et al

docket_entries.description: DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FILED BY DEFENDANT MERMOD, MATTHEW

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MATTHEW MERMOD'S MOTION TO DISMISS PURSUANT TO CCP SEC 583.250(A) FOR FAILURE TO SERVE SUMMONS WITHIN THREE YEARS OF COMMENCEMENT OF THE ACTION; MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE AND VACATE DEFAULT JUDGMENT. THE MOTION TO DISMISS PURSUANT TO CCP 583.250(A) IS DENIED. ESTOPPEL APPLIES. A PERSON MAY NOT LULL ANOTHER INTO A FALSE SENSE OF SECURITY BY CONDUCT CAUSING THE LATTER TO FOREBEAR TO DO SOMETHING WHICH HE OTHERWISE WOULD HAVE DONE AND THEN TAKE ADVANTAGE OF THE INACTION CAUSED BY HIS OWN CONDUCT. (BISS V. BOHR (1995) 40 CAL.APP.4TH 1246, 1252.) DEFENDANT ENTERED INTO DISCUSSIONS WITH PLAINTIFF TO SETTLE THE DEBT, WHICH WOULD HAVE CAUSED PLAINTIFF TO REASONABLY BELIEVE THAT DEFENDANT WAS PROPERLY SERVED. MOTION TO VACATE DEFAULT IS DENIED. A MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM JUDGMENT VALID ON ITS FACE BUT VOID FOR IMPROPER SERVICE IS GOVERNED BY ANALOGY TO CCP 473.5, AND THEREFORE, RELIEF MUST BE SOUGHT NO LATER THAN 2 YEARS AFTER ENTRY OF THE DEFAULT JUDGMENT. (SEE ROGERS V. SILVERMAN (1989) 216 CAL.APP.3D 1114, 1121-1122.) MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS IS DENIED. A DEFAULT JUDGMENT MUST FIRST BE SET ASIDE BEFORE A MOTION TO QUASH MAY BE RULED ON. AS DISCUSSED ABOVE, THE DEFAULT JUDGMENT STANDS AND THEREFORE A MOTION TO QUASH WOULD BE PROCEDURALLY IMPROPER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: LESLIE C. NICHOLS; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion To Determine Party Prevailing On Contract And To Fix Amount Of Attorney'S Fees Awardable As Item Of Costs
Off Calendar Sep-02-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendant Matthew Mermod's Motion to Determine Party Prevailing on Contract and to Fix Amount of Attorney's Fees Awardable as Item of Costs is GRANTED IN PART. Civil Code section 1717 transforms a unilateral contract right to attorneys' fees into a reciprocal provision. (Santisas v. Goodin (1998) 17 Cal.4th 599, 610-11.) This Court may fix what are reasonable attorneys' fees and is not limited by the amount of damages awarded. (Civ. Code 1717; Cruz v. Ayromloo (2007) 155 Cal.App.4th 1270, 1276.) Here, the guaranty contract forming the basis for the underlying action provides for attorneys' fees. Defendant Mermod is the prevailing party because this Court dismissed plaintiff's action with prejudice. Plaintiff had a default judgment entered without properly serving Mermod. This Court awards to Defendant Mermod $10,000 in attorneys' fees at a $250 lodestar for 40 hours of work on trial and appellate litigation. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Dismiss Pursuant To Ccp Sec 583.250(A) For Failure To Serve Summons Within Three Years Of Commencement Of The Action; Mtn To Quash Service And Vacate Default Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-10-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: DOUGLAS PROVENCHER, ESQ., 707.284.2380, FOR PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION INC.
AMY ANDERSON, ESQ., 415.602.8960, FOR DEFENDANT MATTHEW MERMOD.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling re: DEFENDANT MATTHEW MERMOD'S Motion To Dismiss Pursuant To CCP Sec 583.250(A) For Failure To Serve Summons Within Three Years Of Commencement Of The Action; Motion To Quash Service And Vacate Default Judgment. The motion to Dismiss pursuant to CCP 583.250(A) is denied. Estoppel applies. A person may not lull another into a false sense of security by conduct causing the latter to forebear to do something which he otherwise would have done and then take advantage of the inaction caused by his own conduct. (Biss v. Bohr (1995) 40 Cal.App.4th 1246, 1252.) Defendant entered into discussions with Plaintiff to settle the debt, which would have caused Plaintiff to reasonably believe that Defendant was properly served. Motion to Vacate Default is denied. A motion for relief from judgment valid on its face but void for improper service is governed by analogy to CCP 473.5, and therefore, relief must be sought no later than 2 years after entry of the default judgment. (See Rogers v. Silverman (1989) 216 Cal.App.3d 1114, 1121-1122.) Motion to Quash Service of Summons is denied. A default judgment must first be set aside before a motion to quash may be ruled on. As discussed above, the default judgment stands and therefore a motion to quash would be procedurally improper. Order signed in open court. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

Noticeand Motion To Compel Answers To Post-Judgment Discovery And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar May-05-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION INC.'s Motion To Compel Answers To Post-Judgment Discovery is granted. No opposition has been filed. Defendant's failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Defendant is ordered to respond within 15 days of notice of this order. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant in the amount of $340, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. NOT REPORTED.
